appropriate_use: Join order lines to orders by order_id.
Calculate a technical line amount as quantity multiplied by unit_price.

business_evidence.note: Technical ClickHouse comment; business meaning still requires reviewer confirmation.

business_rules.description: Calculate line amount as quantity multiplied by unit_price when this definition fits the use case.

evidence.note: Formula is inferred from technical column names and requires reviewer confirmation.

business_rules.name: Technical line amount

caveats: Business ownership and refresh expectations require reviewer confirmation.

columns.description: One-based position of the line within an order.

evidence.note: Derived from the ClickHouse column comment.

columns.name: line_number
columns.technical_comment: One-based line position within an order

caveats: The ClickHouse relation is logical and is not treated as an enforced foreign key.
columns.description: Identifier used for the logical join to orders.order_id.

evidence.note: Derived from the ClickHouse column comment.

columns.name: order_id
columns.technical_comment: Parent order; logical join to orders.order_id

caveats: Product master semantics require reviewer confirmation.
columns.description: Synthetic product code captured when the order was placed.

evidence.note: Derived from the ClickHouse column comment.

caveats: Return and cancellation handling require reviewer confirmation.

document.description: One technical row per order line represented in the ClickHouse demo dataset.
document.grain: One row per order_id and line_number.
inappropriate_use: Assume product_code is a complete product master record.
purpose: Support product quantity and order-line value analysis in the demo dataset.

document.summary: One technical row per order line represented in the ClickHouse demo dataset. Grain: One row per order_id and line_number.
